summary: Quality signals scoring feature successfully implemented. The computeQualityScore function calculates approval rates from jobMatch history (approved/(approved+rejected)), boosts candidates with ≥70% approval by +5 points, penalizes <30% by -5 points, requires minimum 3 decisions before applying signal, and uses 90-day decay for stale match history. Implementation is clean, well-tested, and integrates correctly with existing recency scoring.

codeQualityIssues.description: Environment variables for quality signals are NOT documented in .env.example. Variables: QUALITY_SIGNAL_DECAY_DAYS, QUALITY_HIGH_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD, QUALITY_LOW_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD, QUALITY_HIGH_APPROVAL_BOOST, QUALITY_LOW_APPROVAL_PENALTY, QUALITY_MIN_DECISIONS

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker added QUALITY_CONFIG to scoring.ts following existing pattern of SCORING_WEIGHTS, HYBRID_BLEND, RECENCY_CONFIG exports. This matches project conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/services/scoring.ts:1-120 — QualityConfig type and export follow same structure as RecencyResult/RecencyConfig

sharedStateObservations.observation: Environment variable configuration values documented in comments within code, but not in .env.example. Should add QUALITY_SIGNAL_* env vars to .env.example under 'Recency/configuratie' section alongside RECENCY_* vars.
Dutch language reasoning strings used consistently (Hoge goedkeuring, Lage goedkeuring). Follows existing convention from recency scoring (Recente match, Verouderde match).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/services/scoring.ts:135-145 — quality reasoning contains Dutch labels matching job context

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skill for backend features follows established procedure: read schema → identify patterns → implement → test → lint → commit.
